rationale: Katalogos has no tenant-continuity constraint (no Tag-Heuer-style migration). Full 5-step brand exercise. Distinct visual identity from Erevna by design — they're sibling indie products, not the same brand.

wordmark.notes: Friendlier geometric than Outfit. Slightly warmer letterforms — fits restaurant hospitality. Differentiates Katalogos from Erevna while staying in the modern-indie-SaaS family. Latin only.

tagline.notes: Direct value-prop framing. Speaks to the SMB restaurant owner's biggest objection ('digital menus sound complicated'). The em-dash gives it a rhythm beat without feeling formal.

palette.name: Terracotta Warm
palette.rationale: Italian trattoria / rustic taverna feel. Clay primary signals warmth and food. Honey accent for highlights. Cream backgrounds keep it inviting rather than aggressive. Pairs well with the Manrope wordmark by giving it a soft, handcrafted context.

usage.primary: Buttons, links, headers, brand-accent UI elements
usage.accent: Focus states, badges, secondary highlights, active indicators

typography.tenantOverrideRationale: Restaurants invest heavily in their printed-menu typography. Letting them carry that typography into their digital menu is a real differentiator vs. competitors that lock the font. Marketing positioning: 'Match your printed menu's typography.' Same code path applies to Erevna tenants for free.

tierMapping.paid-no-ads: 9 fonts (adds IBM Plex, DM Sans, Plus Jakarta, Lora, Source Serif, Newsreader)
tierMapping.paid-full: Full library — 12 fonts (adds Fraunces, Cormorant, Bricolage Grotesque)

typography.fallbackBehavior: Tenants with no font override get Manrope. New feature for Phase 3.5 — does NOT block launch. Tag Heuer migrates with no font field set; visual change-free since Erevna inherits BaseClient defaults and Katalogos tenants are greenfield.
